title: boronia ternata var. elongata
intro: Boronia ternata var. elongata, also known as the Eastern Boronia, is a flowering plant species native to the eastern and southern regions of Australia. The medicinal properties of Boronia ternata var. elongata include its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its essential oil, particularly borneol. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to treat skin conditions like eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of Boronia ternata var. elongata include essential oil, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
